summary: Excellent pre‑FM soundboard capture of the legendary 12/31/72 New Year’s Eve show, highlighted by a monster Truckin’ → The Other One → Morning Dew sequence.
review: This recording delivers a crystal‑clear vault‑reel soundboard mix that many fans describe as pure, clean and even “killer” despite occasional low volume or a “sterile” feel. The performance is consistently praised as one of the band’s all‑time best – Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Phil and Bill lay down a thunderous opening for the epic Truckin’/Other One jam, and the second set rolls into an unforgettable Morning Dew. Across more than two dozen reviews the consensus is that both the music and the audio are top‑shelf.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 25 detailed reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars, praising both musical peaks (Truckin’ → Other One → Morning Dew) and a high‑quality soundboard source; only minor recording complaints appear, yielding an overall rating in the exceptional range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Vault pre‑FM reels are described as pure and clean with great instrument balance; a few listeners note low volume or a slightly “sterile” EQ, but the mix is generally praised as crisp, with no major dropouts.

standout_songs: Truckin' > The Other One > Morning Dew
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely before the high‑energy second set; the centerpiece is a 55‑minute Truckin’/Other One jam that erupts into a soaring Morning Dew, creating a seamless bridge into 1973‑era improvisation.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for blistering, soulful lead work and a “killer” tone during the Truckin’ → Other One transition.
band_member_comments.Phil: Credited with opening the monster The Other One jam with powerful bass lines.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals on Jack Straw called “breath‑taking”; overall rhythm guitar praised but not singled out elsewhere.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming highlighted for driving the long jams and setting up the explosive second‑set flow.
